cards.definition: The typical Biochemical Oxygen Demand (a measure of organic pollution load) of the wastewater produced from processing Arabica coffee.
cards.analogy: A pollution-load reading, the water-treatment equivalent of a blood-sugar test — a number that determines what treatment is actually required.
cards.why_it_matters: The starting point for any effluent treatment conversation — you can't size a lagoon without first knowing this.
cards.cloze: The BOD of Arabica coffee effluent typically ranges between ___ ppm.

cards.definition: The typical Biochemical Oxygen Demand of Robusta processing wastewater — higher than Arabica's, reflecting Robusta's thicker mucilage load.
cards.analogy: A more concentrated version of the same pollution-load problem Arabica processing creates, just with a heavier starting load.
cards.why_it_matters: Means a Robusta-heavy operation needs a larger or more robust treatment system than an equivalent Arabica one.
cards.cloze: The BOD of Robusta coffee effluent typically ranges between ___ ppm, higher than Arabica.

cards.definition: The legal ceiling, under Central Pollution Control Board rules, on how much organic pollution load treated water can carry if it's going to be reused for irrigation.
cards.analogy: A pass/fail bar water has to clear before it's allowed back onto farmland, not a suggestion.
cards.why_it_matters: The number that determines whether treated effluent can legally be reused on the farm at all.
cards.cloze: As per CPCB rules, water used for irrigation should not contain more than ___ ppm of BOD.

cards.definition: The legal ceiling, under CPCB rules, on organic pollution load for water released into a natural stream or water body — a stricter bar than the irrigation-reuse limit.
cards.analogy: A stricter pass/fail bar than the irrigation limit, because a natural water body has no crop to absorb the excess.
cards.why_it_matters: Worth knowing which limit — irrigation or discharge — applies to how a given farm is actually disposing of its effluent.
cards.cloze: Water discharged to natural water bodies should not contain more than ___ ppm of BOD.

cards.definition: The dose of liming material needed to bring acidic coffee effluent back to a near-neutral pH before further treatment or discharge.
cards.analogy: An antacid dose sized to the exact acidity of what it's neutralizing, not a rough pinch.
cards.why_it_matters: The step that makes the rest of the treatment process (lagoons, discharge) actually compliant, not just diluted.
cards.cloze: Coffee effluent is neutralized to a pH of 6.9-7.0 using about ___ grams of liming material per litre.
